summary: Iteration 5 (3 new frontend-only features on top of iteration 4): RouteTransition overlay, Admin CSV Export, Chat Memory Panel. Backend regression: 12/12 pytest pass (root, access CRUD + validation, admin login wrong+correct, admin leads no-auth/bad-token/valid-token, chat stream 422 empty + SSE tokens). Frontend: (a) RouteTransition — on / initial mount overlay stays hidden (class 'route-transition', opacity 0 after 1.5s); navigating page.goto('/admin') flips class to 'route-transition-on' within ~100ms and CSS transition fades opacity back within ~1s; overlay does not block interactions after fade. (b) CSV Export — logged in with 'xenia-admin-2026', clicked data-testid='admin-export-csv'; verified via anchor spy that anchor.download attribute matches /^xenia-waitlist-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.csv$/ (actual: 'xenia-waitlist-2026-07-21.csv'), blob content header exactly = 'id,email,role,company,created_at', data lines = 22 = leads.length; toast 'Exported 22 requests.' shown. Button correctly has 'disabled' logic when leads is empty (React `disabled={!leads || leads.length === 0}`). (c) Chat Memory Panel — on 1920x1080, opened nav-chat; chat-memory-panel visible with empty-state text 'Nothing yet…'. Sent 'Give me a brief on Northlake Robotics and any signals for Solstice or Amelia'; after send: memory-companies shows Northlake Robotics ×1 and Solstice Energy ×1 with industry sub-line, memory-contacts shows Amelia Roux ×1, memory-topics shows 'Brief Northlake Robotics any signals Solsti…'. memory-co-c-northlake and memory-co-c-solstice testids present; clicking memory-co-c-northlake opens data-testid='company-detail' panel. (d) Clear reset — clicking chat-memory-clear rotates localStorage['xenia.chat.session'] to a NEW value (verified xn-chat-ahsinhgc-… → xn-chat-47kieaml-…), chat returns to the seed greeting message, memory panel returns to empty state.
critical_code_review_comments: RouteTransition.jsx — firstRun ref prevents the overlay flashing on the very first hydration, so SplashLoader on / owns the initial paint. Because index.js wraps <App /> in <React.StrictMode>, useEffect double-invokes in dev; the first invocation flips firstRun.current=false, the second invocation shows the overlay. This is why a hard page.goto('/admin') still triggers the overlay in the test environment. In a production build (no StrictMode dev double-render), a fresh /admin load will NOT show the overlay — the transition only fires on in-app pathname changes. This is arguably the intended UX; but flag: there is currently no in-app link between / and /admin (Nav has only anchors + href='#'), so the only real trigger in production is manual URL change or programmatic navigation. Consider adding a Link component or exposing programmatic navigation to make the animation observable end-to-end for real users.

Admin.jsx exportCsv() — Blob is built client-side with proper CSV escaping (wrap in quotes when value contains ,"\n; double-up embedded quotes). Filename stamp uses ISO date slice, timezone-agnostic UTC (toISOString().slice(0,10)); good. URL.revokeObjectURL called after click. Button disabled when leads empty. Non-blocking: `new Date(r.created_at).toISOString()` will throw if server ever returns a null created_at — currently it's always populated by the model default, so safe.

chatMemory.js — extractMemory scans both user & assistant messages for known company/contact names (case-insensitive, includes short-form matching e.g. 'Northlake'), and derives topics only from user messages with stopword removal. Deterministic and cheap. topics.slice(-6).reverse() caps to 6 most recent — good. Regex `\b${first.toLowerCase()}\b` for contact first-name could be false-positive on very short first names — none in the current data set (shortest is 'Frida'), so fine.

AIChat.jsx resetConversation() — aborts in-flight fetch, rotates session_id in localStorage, resets msgs to SEED, resets q & streaming. This is the *right* order (abort → rotate → reset UI), so the backend chat session is genuinely started fresh on next send. Good.

Non-blocking: AIChat.jsx layout — sidebar is `hidden lg:flex` (visible ≥ 1024px). At <lg the sidebar is intentionally hidden but there is no fallback (e.g., a mobile 'memory' drawer or button) — acceptable for MVP; noted in spec.

Non-blocking: RouteTransition CSS transition duration is 260ms but the JS hide timer fires at 620ms. That gives ~880ms total overlay lifetime. The spec says 'fades back to opacity 0 within ~700ms' — during our test at t=1s post-goto the overlay had class removed but opacity was still ~0.75 mid-fade. It DOES reach 0 by ~880ms. Not a bug, but if the intent is a total ~700ms lifetime, either shorten JS timer to 440ms or shorten CSS transition.
seed_data_creation: A handful of test_<uuid>@example.com AccessRequest rows per pytest run (harmless waitlist entries). Waitlist currently has 22 rows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 new features pass. IMPORTANT for RouteTransition testing: Playwright's `page.on('download')` returns a random-uuid `suggested_filename` for anchor-triggered blob URLs after the anchor is immediately removed from DOM (Admin.jsx does document.body.removeChild(a) after click). To verify the download attribute robustly, spy on HTMLAnchorElement.prototype.click BEFORE triggering the export and read `this.getAttribute('download')` at click time — that's what a real user's browser sees. For Chat Memory tests, always use viewport >= 1024x for the panel to be visible (lg breakpoint). Session id key: 'xenia.chat.session'; admin token keys: 'xenia.admin.token' + 'xenia.admin.exp'; OS memory: 'xenia.os.state'.
